raw_text: alejandro morales blasco operations engineering manager air horizont camino cabaldós 9, 42a, zaragoza, 50013, spain * morales20blas@hotmail.com * +34628593400 +44 7523652961 linkedin: www. linkedin.com/in/mbalejandro personal profile graduated in aeronautical and aerospace engineering at the university of leeds, currently working as an operations engineering manager at air horizont and passionate about engineering currently looking to get involved in the aerospace sector. highly motivated, eager to learn and committed to personal and professional goals, holding the ability to work effectively within a team as well as individually, with excellent communication and problem-solving skills, good adaptability and ability to deliver under pressure, with experience in cad modelling, running cfd simulations, and programming matlab codes. education university of leeds leeds, england beng, meng aeronautical 8: aerospace engineering 2017 2021 awarded with 1% class degree, achieved outstanding results in my degree as shown below: level 1 (74.8%): engineering mathematics, solid mechanics, thermofluids. level 2 (78.83%): mechatronics, economics, vibration and control, e. mechanics. level 3 (72.2%): aerodynamics and aerospace propulsion, aerospace flight mechanics. level 4 (78.6%): cfd analysis, design optimisation, aerospace structures. ies francisco grande covian zaragoza, spain a-levels in technological sciences 2015 - 2017 awarded with honours by getting the highest promotion grades, obtaining an average of 97.2%. work experience air horizont ltd zaragoza, spain operations engineering manager and efb administrator full time mar 2022 - present e electronic flight bags (efb) administrator: managing access and instructor of applications used by pilots e responsible of technical office: supervising operational documentation to comply with regulations: aircraft manufacturer and operation manuals, technical projects affecting operations. e weight and balance: prepare and ensure flight load-sheets are correctly calculated considering center of gravity shifts due to cabin movements or fuel consumption, contributing to flight safety e assisting the safety manager in safety related tasks such as update of navigation databases or flight data. e performing same tasks as shown in the operations engineer internship description operations engineer internship sep 2021 mar 2022 e routes studies using flight planification software to predict aircraft performance during flight phases. e airport research, categorization, and briefing elaboration for pilots e assisting compliance monitoring manager and safety manager to carry out technical office tasks ensuring conformity with relevant regulations and flight safety. santo domingo juventud zaragoza, spain football coach sep 2016 - june 2017 e coached u-10 team: responsible for workout planning and execution, and assistant to principal coach. e organised trainings, assisted on match preparation and travel logistics. finished top 5 in the local league engineering projects university of leeds level 4 sep 2020 may 2021 e thesis, team project. developed matlab model to conduct hydrodynamics and vehicle dynamics analysis of crusader k6. innovative adaptation of savitsky method to achieve 76% on technical report and presentation. university of leeds level 3 sep 2019 may 2020 e aerospace vehicle design. collaborated in aerodynamic preliminary design of small uav for surveillance applications, by performing trade off studies, numerical analysis and 2d aerofoil simulations using xfoil. e individual engineering project. working independently for the formula student team to improve drag/downforce ratio using cfd tool ansys, achieving 72% in the final report. university of leeds level 2 sep 2018 may 2019 e martian mile team of 7. manufactured a mars rover from scratch, took the lead in design and building tasks, and assembled suspension and chassis, reaching fourth position. technical skills ansys matlab/simulink solidworks abaqus microsoft office key employability skills it skills - meng/beng course, air horizont e experience using microsoft office tools to carry out analysis, write technical reports and present conclusions. communication, teamwork football, engineering projects e developed during 10 years of playing team sports, in particular football. e challenged during year 4 team project, due to social distancing, it was crucial to share, discuss, and conclude the findings of each member working in different roles with various study areas. problem solving, creativity thesis, air horizont e shaped innovative solutions to complex problems, especially when adapting savitsky method to the crusader. e taking the lead on chassis and suspension design of mars rover. flexibility, adaptability engineering projects e ability to work long hard hours, required for carrying out an individual project with own personal goals e adaptability to new situations, as did pursuing a professional career and degree outside my native country. leadership, time management football, engineering projects, air horizont e = acquired during football coaching time, captain experience and enhanced by leading design tasks at martian mile. organisation skills for setting up work route and objectives for individual and team projects. e managing operations department, efb administrator and instructor while completing technical office projects. languages e english (c1) : spanish (native) interests 8. achievements e motorsport fan, formula 1, and motogp, and member of motorsport society e keen sportsman, particularly football, tennis, golf and karting. gym member for the past five years. e football player (2006 - 2017) captain for three years. e football league (2008, 2017) and torneo fco. goya (2010) champion in zaragoza. references academic reference dr carl gilkeson, university of leeds, school of mechanical engineering email: c.a.gilkeson@leeds.ac.uk tel: +44 (0) 113 343 6915
